sourceMovie.title: Once Upon a Time in the West
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Once Upon a Time in the West works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and western blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: News of the World
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Once Upon a Time in the West clicked for you, News of the World (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and western, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Once Upon a Time in the West?
faq.answer: Try News of the World, Rio Bravo, Appaloosa—each shares DNA with Once Upon a Time in the West while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Once Upon a Time in the West?
faq.answer: Once Upon a Time in the West (1968) is best described as Drama, Western. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Once Upon a Time in the West worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
